rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARY SWAYNE MARTIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing; however, the changes to the attorney costs were an amendment, the executor had time to meet with estate sale companies but did not have time to inventory the tangible personal, even if it had no value (or she should have addressed this in her report without the Court questioning expenditures for non-existent assets), beneficiaries are not necessarily heirs, so the allegation in paragraph 8B of the petition was incomplete, and Probate Code Section 13100 affidavits should have been filed by the successors-in-interest to the post-deceased beneficiaries. In light of the fact that no distribution might otherwise have been made to the residuary beneficiaries, the absence of 13100 affidavits will be waived for this petition.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BESSIE L. FRANKLIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (1470 Palou Avenue, San Francisco, Ca 94124)
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, despite the following issues: 1) Items 1a & 2e: No legal description is included in Attachment 2e. 2) Item 4a: No manner of vesting title was specified (e.g., an unmarried woman, a married woman as her sole and separate property, etc.). 3) Item 4c: The reported sale was made (offer/bid accepted and returned to Court for confirmation) on 04/10/2024, BEFORE notice of sale was first published on 05/21/2024, and BEFORE the deadline specified in the published notice for submitting offers/bids. See Probate Code section 10306(a)("A private sale of real property may not be made before the day stated in the notice of sale as the day on or after which the sale will be made"); see also LRSF 14.31B("No offer can be accepted until the date on or after the time for making bids expires."). A report of sale and petition to confirm the sale cannot be filed until AFTER completion of publication of notice and acceptance of an original offer/bid. 4) Item 5b: The second box ("exclusive") was not checked. Also, the name of the responsible broker for the seller (but not the salesperson without a broker license) must be specified (e.g., Infinity Investments, Steve Peterson, etc.). 5) Item 8b: There is one request for special notice on file. 6) Attachment 4f: The published notice stated that the property would be sold on an "as-is" basis, but Attachment 4f did not. See section 10307 & LRSF 14.31(C). 7) Item 12b of the proposed order: The commission calculation is incorrect. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARY K. MORITZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/6/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) In her initial petition for letters of administration, the proposed administrator did not identify the decedent's parents and their dates of death, as required by LRSF 14.18 C.3. This should now be set forth for the record. (2) Notice must have ATTACHED to it a printout of the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] In any event, the separately mailed Notice of Directions for Remote Appearance and Access shows that notice was mailed to Robert J. Moritz at an address other than the one reported in the petition to be his. (3) His address changed again on the proof of service of the attorney's "supplemental declaration" filed on 5/3/24. (4) The petition for final distribution must state the date on which notice to the Franchise Tax Board was mailed pursuant to Probate Code Sect, 9202(c). [LRSF 14.35 I.] (5) A petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed by showing the distributee's name and description of the property, including the complete description of each individual security distributable to each beneficiary. [CRC 7.651; LRSF 14.36 I.6.a.] A percentage interest in the estate assets is not sufficient. (The date of death valuation of securities does not need to be included in the proposed distribution, as that value changes.) (6) How will the attorney fees and costs be paid when there is no cash in the estate? Please provide a revised proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 7/23/24. It appears that an amendment to the petition should be filed. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Item 4 of an I&A should NOT be completed when a probate referee HAS BEEN appointed. In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate and distribute an estate must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution." [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.] Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).])

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE LIVING TRUST OF THE BASSO FAMILY
rulings.Calendar Matter: First And Final Account And Report Of Successor Trustee; And Petition For Order: 1) Settling And Approving Account And Report; 2) Approving Successor Trustees Fees; 3) Approving Attorneys Fees And Costs; And 4) For Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The accounting period exceeds this Court's limits for a first accounting period or subsequent accounting periods. The proposed distribution of the remaining $2,902.39 is to subtrusts for the trustors' four children, but to the GST Exempt Trusts for all but Ray Basso Jr., whose trust is just shown as the "Exempt Trust." There has been nothing to verify the starting assets (reported as "Additions to Trust"). In the absence of an I&A or formal appraisals, the Court must rely solely on the trustee's allegation of the starting values. It is unclear how L. Theodore Scheley, III, became the successor co-trustee with Wells Fargo Bank. He resigned on 6/5/17, but nothing has been filed to indicate why Christian Scheley was not the initial co-trustee with Wells Fargo Bank; Christian Scheley was the first named successor co-trustee. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ROGER HENRY PEDEUPE REVOCABLE TRUST, DATED 5/23/18, AS AMENDED
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Appointing Successor Trustee; Determining The Validity Of Trust Provisions; And For Instructions
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/30/2024. The following issues to be supplemented two weeks prior to the date: (1) Notice of Hearing with a copy of petition to the settlor/current beneficiary, Roger Henry Pedeupe. (2) Petition to provide a complete family tree of the relatives (first cousins once removed) so that the court can ensure notices have been given properly to all persons who are entitled to. (3) Need allegation why the Personal Property Gift Letter (dated 7/6/21) cannot/should not be considered as the Will, especially the document itself sets forth so on its face. Prob. Code sec. 6110(c)(2). (4) Need a copy of "Exhibit A" to which the Trust (i.e. Division 1) refers. (5) Need further allegation as to the settlor's legal capacity for a judicial determination of incompetency. Prob. Code sec. 15800(c) as the trust is silent as to how or who to determine the settlor's incompetency. (6) If Petitioner can provide sufficient evidence for lack of legal capacity of the settlor, why GAL should or could not be appointed for him. (7) Further allegation of legal capacity of the settlor at the time of the Gift Letter being executed. (8) Further supporting allegation regarding the Gift Letter. For example, who prepared the document, if the preparer has any further supporting allegation. (9) The fair market value of the trust asset for the full bond. Prob. Code sec. 15602(a)(3). Petition alleges that the real property is the trust asset; however, seeking the minimum bond only. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LINDA G. GUZZARDI
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continue to 7/9/24. A verified supplement clearing the below issues to be filed one week prior to the date: Some beneficiaries predeceased the decedent, but their surviving issues are unclear. (1) Lamar F. Guyton (brother) - survived by any issue? (2) Helen Barker (aunt) - survived by any issue? (3) Paul F. Leachman, Sr (step-father) - his two children, Paul Jr. and Mary- are also the named $10K beneficiaries. It is not certain whether Paul Jr. and Mary are the only issues of Paul Sr. (4) Jan Barker (cousin) - Jan's daughter Jenna Phillips is also a named It is not certain whether Jenna is the only issue of Jan Barker. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LINDA GORDON HOWARD
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/6/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Neither the mailed nor published notice indicated that a (holographic) will was to be admitted. (2) When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] Who were the decedent's parents and when did they die? (3) A proposed non-resident personal representative must file a declaration setting forth in detail the anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate for the Court to determine the amount of the bond. [LRSF 14.16 F.] (4) When a holographic instrument is offered for probate, it must always be accompanied by an exact typewritten copy. Where the petitioner is also the sole beneficiary, a Proof of Holographic Instrument is more persuasive if signed by a disinterested party. [LRSF 14.18 A.] Since there apparently were witnesses, it might be more persuasive to file a Proof of Subscribing Witness Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 7/23/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Ite 3g(2) of a Petition for Letters of Administration with Will Annexed should not be completed when there is a will.)
